title: lithocarpus phansipanensis
intro: Lithocarpus phansipanensis, also known as the Himalayan Lithocarpus, is a deciduous tree species in the beech family, native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Lithocarpus phansipanensis include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, Lithocarpus phansipanensis is often used in the form of teas, decoctions, or infusions, and its bark is also used to make traditional medicines.
